title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Program Brief: Invalidation
summary: Changing a layout rebuilds one page, deleting content leaves output behind, cached nodes ignore parser versions, watch events duplicate builds, and a fast incremental result differs from a clean build. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the invalidation increment in clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-invalidation — Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_incremental_dependencies_4(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:

    if stale_outputs > 0:

    if not clean_digest_equal:

    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"

Competency probe: Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.

steps.why: Retrieving Invalidation through the concrete clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Invalidation model · Theory Invalidation, keep Invalidation connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Invalidation model · Theory Invalidation, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Invalidation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Invalidation model · Theory Invalidation, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7042 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Invalidation model · Theory Invalidation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Incremental invalidation only needs to handle edited files.
